title: betula glandulosa var. sibirica
intro: Betula glandulosa var. sibirica, also known as the Sibirian birch, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to northeastern Asia and the Russian Far East. The bark of this plant contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides medicinal properties that can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. The health benefits of Betula glandulosa var. sibirica include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use in traditional medicine to stimulate circulation and improve immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the bark in hot water or oil to extract its medicinal compounds.
